reviews.review_description: Wow, sooooo dang addicting. Very clever puzzles and physics make this game nearly impossible to put down. $6 feels like an absolute steal. Get this before the price inevitably increases (as it should). This is a top 10 puzzler for me on quest, and I’ve played em ALL.

A very addictive puzzler, where you throw a Frisbee to clones of yourself to progress through levels. The game concept and level design deserves a 5 star rating, in the current state its 4 stars for me, because there is still room for improvement, like the disc could be rendered with a higher framerate.

After watching the trailer, I thought this game would be right up my alley, and it absolutely is. It has a minimalist, futuristic vibe with a focus on throwing discs through tight puzzle environments.

The gameplay initially feels a bit like rubbing your belly and patting your head at the same time. It’s a little awkward for the first few minutes of the tutorial, but once you find your rhythm, you’re set. The game does a great job of teaching you as you go.

I’m still working my way through the campaign, but there is an arcade mode available once you finish. The core loop involves throwing a disc to another version of yourself within a maze, requiring you to manage enemies, angles and precise timing. The checkpoints are fair, which keeps the challenge from becoming frustrating. Pro tip: you’ll want a bit of physical space to try and reach for your bad throws. It’s not necessary, but it definitely helps.

A few minor wishes for future updates: Slightly snappier load times between throw resets. A leaderboard or grading system (time taken, throw count, or a star rating) to track progress. A level select. A more interesting "exit/end" rather than just a plain white screen.

These are minor complaints for an otherwise great experience. If you enjoy puzzles, this is a cool game that is absolutely worth the $5 price tag.
Very addicting - "just one more level before I quit" and here I am 3 hours later! Really chill and immersive game.
